monarchs.name: William I (the Conqueror)
monarchs.key_fact: Won the Battle of Hastings in 1066; commissioned the Domesday Book

monarchs.name: William II
monarchs.key_fact: Third son of William I; killed in a hunting accident in the New Forest

monarchs.name: Henry I
monarchs.key_fact: Youngest son of William I; established the Charter of Liberties

monarchs.name: Stephen
monarchs.key_fact: His reign saw civil war (The Anarchy) with Matilda

monarchs.name: Henry II
monarchs.key_fact: Established common law; conflict with Thomas Becket

monarchs.name: Richard I (the Lionheart)
monarchs.key_fact: Spent most of his reign on Crusades; famous warrior king

monarchs.name: John
monarchs.key_fact: Forced to sign the Magna Carta in 1215

monarchs.name: Henry III
monarchs.key_fact: Rebuilt Westminster Abbey; faced baronial opposition

monarchs.name: Edward I
monarchs.key_fact: Conquered Wales; attempted to conquer Scotland

monarchs.name: Edward II
monarchs.key_fact: Defeated by Robert the Bruce at Bannockburn (1314)

monarchs.name: Edward III
monarchs.key_fact: Started the Hundred Years War with France; Black Death struck 1348

monarchs.name: Richard II
monarchs.key_fact: Faced the Peasants' Revolt of 1381; deposed by Henry IV

monarchs.name: Henry IV
monarchs.key_fact: First Lancastrian king; took the throne from Richard II

monarchs.name: Henry V
monarchs.key_fact: Won the Battle of Agincourt in 1415

monarchs.name: Henry VI
monarchs.key_fact: Lost the Hundred Years War; his reign saw the Wars of the Roses begin

monarchs.name: Edward IV
monarchs.key_fact: First Yorkist king; established relative peace and prosperity

monarchs.name: Edward V
monarchs.key_fact: One of the Princes in the Tower; reigned only 2 months

monarchs.name: Richard III
monarchs.key_fact: Last Plantagenet king; killed at the Battle of Bosworth Field

monarchs.name: Henry VII
monarchs.key_fact: Won Battle of Bosworth; united Lancaster and York by marrying Elizabeth of York

monarchs.name: Henry VIII
monarchs.key_fact: Had 6 wives; broke with Rome and established the Church of England

monarchs.name: Edward VI
monarchs.key_fact: Son of Henry VIII; Protestant reforms continued

monarchs.name: Mary I (Bloody Mary)
monarchs.key_fact: Restored Catholicism; persecuted Protestants

monarchs.name: Elizabeth I
monarchs.key_fact: The Virgin Queen; defeated the Spanish Armada in 1588; golden age of English culture

monarchs.name: James I (James VI of Scotland)
monarchs.key_fact: United the crowns of England and Scotland; Gunpowder Plot 1605

monarchs.name: Charles I
monarchs.key_fact: English Civil War; executed in 1649

monarchs.name: Oliver Cromwell (Lord Protector)
monarchs.key_fact: Led the Commonwealth; not a king but ruled as Lord Protector

monarchs.name: Charles II
monarchs.key_fact: The Restoration; Great Fire of London 1666

monarchs.name: James II
monarchs.key_fact: Catholic king; overthrown in the Glorious Revolution

monarchs.name: William III & Mary II
monarchs.key_fact: Glorious Revolution; Bill of Rights 1689

monarchs.name: Anne
monarchs.key_fact: Act of Union 1707 united England and Scotland

monarchs.name: George I
monarchs.key_fact: First Hanoverian king; spoke little English

monarchs.name: George III
monarchs.key_fact: Lost the American colonies; Napoleonic Wars

monarchs.name: Victoria
monarchs.key_fact: Longest-reigning monarch until Elizabeth II; British Empire at its peak

monarchs.name: George V
monarchs.key_fact: Changed the royal family name from Saxe-Coburg-Gotha to Windsor in 1917

monarchs.name: Edward VIII
monarchs.key_fact: Abdicated to marry Wallis Simpson

monarchs.name: George VI
monarchs.key_fact: Led Britain through World War II

monarchs.name: Elizabeth II
monarchs.key_fact: Longest-reigning British monarch (70 years)

monarchs.name: Charles III
monarchs.key_fact: Current reigning monarch
